stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the Meta-Llama-3-8B model, a significant LLM from Meta.
It is presented via Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating its performance tracking in the ML community.
The context is focused on text-generation tasks within the transformers ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the core area of text generation using transformer-based architectures.
The mention of Llama and Hugging Face indicates relevance within the current LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: GPIC: A Giant Permissive Image Corpus for Visual Generation
stories.author: Li Fei-Fei
bullet_points: Introduction of GPIC, a massive image corpus totaling approximately 28 trillion pixels, designed for visual generative modeling.
The corpus is highly accessible, featuring 100M training examples and is permissively licensed for both research and commercial use.
GPIC is engineered for quality, having undergone safety filtering and deduplication, and is captioned by a state-of-the-art vision-language model.

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: Introduces SchGen, a novel large language model designed to generate editable PCB schematics.
Addresses a critical bottleneck in electronics design: the manual and expertise-intensive nature of PCB schematic creation.
Pioneers the use of LLMs for this task by overcoming challenges related to suitable code representations and dataset scarcity.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, indicating a focus on practical deployment.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline indicates the model is being used for text generation tasks.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware for optimized AI inference.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs directly on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing AI assistance while maintaining user data privacy through local execution.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows users to run various coding models (e.g., Claude Code, Codex) locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being deployed in partnership with ROOST.
The models are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, promoting open-source safety tooling.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: LLMSurgeon addresses the lack of transparency regarding the pretraining data mixture ('digital DNA') of Large Language Models (LLMs).
The work formalizes Data Mixture Surgery (DMS), allowing researchers to estimate the domain-level distribution of an LLM's training corpus using only generated text.
This capability is crucial for post-hoc auditing, understanding model behaviors, and diagnosing failure modes in LLMs.

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: SchGen is a novel LLM designed to generate editable Printed Circuit Board (PCB) schematics directly from natural language requests.
The research addresses a major bottleneck in hardware design, which is currently manual and highly expertise-dependent.
The key technical hurdle overcome was developing an LLM-suited representation and addressing the lack of large-scale datasets for this domain.

stories.title: Unlocking the Working Memory of Large Language Models for Latent Reasoning
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The research addresses a limitation in LLMs where reasoning is tied to external, autoregressive token generation.
It proposes a method inspired by human cognition to use internal 'working memory' for information manipulation.
The goal is to decouple internal computation from external communication to improve LLM reasoning capabilities.

stories.title: Locally Coherent, Globally Incoherent: Bounding Compositional Incoherence in Multi-Component LLM Agents
stories.author: Anany Kotawala
bullet_points: Addresses a critical failure mode in multi-component LLM agents: local coherence does not guarantee global probabilistic coherence.
Introduces a formal measure, the 'compositional residual eps*,' to quantify this global incoherence.
Provides a runtime method to compute this residual using system output and declared cross-component coupling constraints.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: LLMSurgeon addresses the lack of transparency regarding the pretraining data mixture of Large Language Models (LLMs).
The research formalizes 'Data Mixture Surgery (DMS)' to estimate the domain-level distribution of an LLM's corpus.
The tool allows auditing of an LLM's 'digital DNA' using only its generated text, aiding in understanding its behaviors and failure modes.

stories.title: SoundnessBench: Can Your AI Scientist Really Tell Good Research Ideas from Bad Ones?
stories.author: Furong Huang
bullet_points: Introduces SoundnessBench, a new benchmark designed to test if AI models can judge the methodological viability of research ideas.
Addresses a critical bottleneck in autonomous AI research agents: the ability to filter poor research proposals early in the pipeline.
The benchmark is constructed from 1,099 machine learning research proposals originally submitted to ICLR.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: LLMSurgeon addresses the lack of transparency regarding the pretraining data composition of Large Language Models (LLMs).
The work formalizes 'Data Mixture Surgery (DMS)' to estimate the domain-level distribution of an LLM's training corpus.
This allows for post-hoc auditing of LLM behavior and failure modes based solely on generated text.

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: Introduces SchGen, a novel large language model designed to generate editable PCB schematics.
Addresses a significant industry bottleneck: the manual and expertise-intensive nature of PCB design.
Overcomes key challenges by developing LLM-suited representations for schematic generation from natural language.

stories.title: Locally Coherent, Globally Incoherent: Bounding Compositional Incoherence in Multi-Component LLM Agents
stories.author: Anany Kotawala
bullet_points: Addresses a fundamental flaw in multi-component LLM agents where local coherence does not guarantee global correctness.
Introduces a formal metric, the compositional residual (eps*), to quantify this 'locally coherent, globally incoherent' failure.
Provides a runtime method to compute this incoherence based on system output and defined cross-component constraints.

stories.title: MedCase-Structured: A Text-to-FHIR Dataset for Benchmarking Diagnostic Reasoning in Clinically Realistic EHR Settings
stories.author: Ziquan Fu
bullet_points: Introduces MedCase-Structured, a new dataset designed to benchmark diagnostic reasoning in realistic Electronic Health Record (EHR) settings.
Addresses a critical gap in LLM evaluation by generating clinically realistic HL7 FHIR R4 bundles from unstructured text.
Aims to enable controllable evaluation of clinical decision support systems using industry-standard, structured data formats.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released as an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents can run entirely on the user's local hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage tools like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary models like Claude.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a working directory.
Users can leverage various open-weight alternatives (e.g., gpt-oss:20b, gpt-oss:120b) instead of proprietary models.

stories.title: Tech companies desperately want to film you doing chores
stories.author: Robert Hart
bullet_points: An AI training startup named Shift is offering free home cleaning services in New York.
The service requires users to provide video footage of them performing chores.
Shift plans to expand its service model to other major cities, such as London.

stories.title: Botnet of more than 17 million devices dismantled
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A large-scale botnet, comprising over 17 million devices, has been successfully dismantled.
The infrastructure supporting the botnet was reportedly linked to a Russia-based residential proxy network.
The story addresses cybersecurity threats, a consistently relevant topic in tech news.

stories.title: Startup offers free home cleaning—if it can record it all for robot training
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: A startup is offering free home cleaning services in exchange for data collection.
The core requirement is that workers must wear head cameras to record their activities for robot training.
This highlights the ongoing trend of using human labor to generate complex, real-world data for AI/robotics development.

stories.title: Here's why the failure of Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket is so catastrophic
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: The article discusses the potential catastrophic failure of Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket.
The focus is on the high stakes and potential risks associated with the rocket's failure, specifically pad damage.
This falls under the aerospace/space technology sector, indicating moderate industry relevance.

stories.title: Physics Is All You Need? A Case Study in Physicist-Supervised AI Development of Scientific Software
stories.author: Nhat-Minh Nguyen
bullet_points: The study investigates the role of AI agents (Claude Code, Sonnet, Opus) when supervised by a physicist to develop scientific software (CLAX-PT).
It provides a quantified case study detailing 15 supervision events, classifying how the AI progressed autonomously or required expert intervention.
The findings suggest AI agents can resolve tasks autonomously through iteration against tests, supplemented by the physicist's domain knowledge.

stories.title: VideoMLA: Low-Rank Latent KV Cache for Minute-Scale Autoregressive Video Diffusion
stories.author: Pinar Yanardag
bullet_points: Introduces VideoMLA, a new approach to Multi-Head Latent Attention (MLA) in video diffusion models.
Addresses the memory and latency challenges associated with the per-head KV cache in long-rollout causal video diffusion.
Replaces traditional per-head keys and values with a shared, low-rank latent representation.

stories.title: Tiny but Trusted: Efficient Vision-Language Reasoning for Time-Series Anomaly Detection
stories.author: Ismini Lourentzou
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of current Vision-Language Models (VLMs) in detecting anomalies within sequential (time-series) data.
Highlights a key research gap: existing anomaly detection benchmarks lack natural-language rationales, hindering VLM interpretability.
Introduces VisAnomBench, a new benchmark designed to facilitate grounded and interpretable anomaly detection using VLMs.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: A new method, using a 'power distribution' derived from a base model, can achieve comparable reasoning to models trained with Reinforcement Learning (RL).
This approach bypasses the need for expensive post-training steps like RL fine-tuning, curated datasets, or external verifiers.
The primary technical hurdle for practical application is developing an efficient sampler capable of accurately drawing from this power distribution.

stories.title: DynaFLIP: Rethinking Robotics Perception via Tri-Modal-Dynamics Guided Representation
stories.author: Furong Huang
bullet_points: DynaFLIP is a new dynamics-aware multimodal pre-training framework designed to improve robot perception.
It addresses a limitation in current robotics pipelines where motion understanding is often left to downstream policies after static visual encoding.
The framework integrates image, language, and 3D flow triplets to push motion understanding upstream into the perception stage.

stories.title: Efficient Test-Time Finetuning of LLMs via Convex Reconstruction and Gradient Caching
stories.author: Alaa Maalouf
bullet_points: The paper addresses the computational bottleneck of Test-Time Finetuning (TTFT) for Large Language Models (LLMs), where adaptation happens per query.
Existing TTFT methods face a trade-off between speed (fast retrieval) and quality (diverse selection), with strong methods being too costly.
The proposed method, HullFT, aims to improve the efficiency of TTFT by leveraging Convex Reconstruction and Gradient Caching.

stories.title: Fairness-Aware Federated Learning with Trajectory Shapley Value
stories.author: Ziqi Wang
bullet_points: Addresses limitations in Federated Learning (FL) regarding biased and unstable learning due to fixed aggregation weights.
Proposes the Trajectory Shapley Value (TSV) to improve fairness and stability in distributed model training.
Focuses on improving FL's ability to handle heterogeneous and privacy-sensitive data by fairly weighting client contributions.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: A new method proposes achieving strong reasoning in language models by sampling from a 'power distribution' derived from the base model.
This approach potentially bypasses the need for expensive post-training techniques like Reinforcement Learning (RL) or curated datasets.
The primary technical challenge identified is developing an efficient sampler capable of accurately mixing to this specialized power distribution.

stories.title: In-Context Reward Adaptation for Robust Preference Modeling
stories.author: Ermin Wei
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation in Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) where static reward models fail to capture diverse human preferences.
The core problem is that single reward models lack the robustness to generalize to unseen or heterogeneous human preference domains.
The proposed method, In-Context Reward Adaptation, aims to allow models to adapt to new human distributions without requiring costly retraining.

stories.title: Unlocking the Working Memory of Large Language Models for Latent Reasoning
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The research addresses a limitation in LLM reasoning where intermediate steps are tied to autoregressive token generation.
It proposes a method inspired by human cognition to use internal 'working memory' for information manipulation.
The goal is to decouple internal computation from external communication to improve LLM reasoning capabilities.

stories.title: COMPOSE: Composing Future Theorems from Citations and Formal Structure
stories.author: Michael Werman
bullet_points: Introduces 'COMPOSE,' a new method for generating plausible future mathematical theorems.
The model addresses a gap in existing work by simultaneously considering both prior research direction and formal structural constraints.
This grounded generation aims to create mathematically sound and well-motivated claims based on anchor papers.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: A new technique, 'power distribution' sampling, achieves comparable reasoning performance to RL-tuned frontier models.
This method bypasses the need for extensive post-training, curated datasets, or external verifiers.
The primary technical hurdle for practical application is efficiently sampling from this derived power distribution.

stories.title: Resolution Diagnostics for Paired LLM Evaluation
stories.author: Anany Kotawala
bullet_points: The research addresses a methodological issue in LLM evaluation, finding that many existing pairwise rankings on public leaderboards are statistically 'unresolved'.
Specific findings indicate that 11 of 40 comparisons on the Open LLM Leaderboard v1 and 4 of 9 MMLU-Pro adjacent-rank pairs failed to meet a standard resolution target.
The paper proposes framing paired LLM evaluation as a hypothesis testing problem to improve the reliability of comparative LLM assessments.

stories.title: Liquid AI reveals 8B-A1B MoE trained on 38T
stories.author: simjnd
bullet_points: Liquid AI has released a new model, the 8B-A1B.
The model utilizes a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture.
It was trained on a substantial dataset of 38 Trillion tokens.

stories.title: How Model Distillation Actually Works (and What the 'China Distilled Our Model' Headlines Really Mean)
stories.author: p0rt
bullet_points: Provides a practical, non-hype explanation of the mechanics of knowledge distillation in Large Language Models (LLMs).
Addresses the nuances of distilling models from closed APIs, distinguishing it from standard training.
Analyzes and clarifies the context behind recent industry discussions regarding model distillation, such as those involving OpenAI, Anthropic, and DeepSeek.

stories.title: VS Code Prompt Files - Custom Slash Commands for GitHub Copilot
stories.author: petermilovcik
bullet_points: Introduces 'Prompt Files' for VS Code users.
Allows users to convert repetitive GitHub Copilot chat requests into custom slash commands.
Enhances developer workflow efficiency when interacting with Copilot.

stories.title: Code Review When Half the Diffs Are From Agents
stories.author: tacoda
bullet_points: The article discusses the changing nature of code review in the age of AI-generated code.
It posits that traditional code review models, designed for human-authored diffs, are becoming obsolete.
The core theme is the need to adapt software development practices to integrate AI agent contributions.
